question: Is the original bronze sculpture completely replaced by the colorful glass sculpture from the donor image?
Does the inserted sculpture retain the visual identity of the donor object, including its intertwined tubular loops and internal red/blue/yellow coloring?
Does the inserted sculpture appear to be made of transparent glass with internal refraction, rather than opaque material?
Is the rough stone pedestal preserved in its original shape, texture, and color?
Are the gallery background elements (walls, floor, track lights, and wall text) unchanged aside from the new sculpture and caustics?
Is the glass sculpture scaled appropriately to fit entirely on the pedestal top without overhanging unnaturally?
Does the perspective and orientation of the glass sculpture match the gallery view (viewed slightly from below and to the right)?
Have the harsh studio lighting highlights on the glass sculpture been softened to match the diffused gallery spotlight environment?
Is there a realistic contact shadow where the glass sculpture meets the top surface of the pedestal?
Are subtle caustic light patterns visible on the floor around the base of the pedestal?
Is the direction of the caustic patterns consistent with the spotlight source coming from the upper right?
Are the caustic patterns subtle and integrated into the floor texture, rather than appearing as an opaque or overly bright overlay?
Are the edges of the glass sculpture clean and free from white fringing, halos, or 'cut-out' artifacts against the background?
Does the noise and grain level of the inserted glass sculpture match the film-like quality of the original gallery photo?
Is the overall color tone of the glass sculpture and caustics cohesive with the neutral tones of the gallery environment?
Is the image free from ghosting artifacts, remnants of the original bronze sculpture, or black background contamination from the donor image?
Is the glass sculpture free from structural distortions or unnatural deformations of its tubular forms?
If floor reflections are visible, are they subtle and consistent with the polished concrete surface texture?
